title: 10 best herbal creams for gum swelling

remedies.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis creams helps with gum swelling because of its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The cream's active compounds, such as triterpenoid saponins and flavonoids, work together to reduce inflammation and prevent infection. By promoting healing and soothing the affected area, Calendula cream can alleviate gum swelling and discomfort. Additionally, its moisturizing properties help to keep the gums hydrated and healthy, promoting a speedy recovery and preventing further irritation. This natural remedy is often used to treat gum inflammation.
remedy_constituents: Sesquiterpene lactones: These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help reduce gum swelling and prevent infection.
Flavonoids: Flavonoids, such as kaempferol and quercetin, exhibit strong anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help alleviate gum swelling and promote healing.

Carotenoids (specifically, Calendula carotenoid): Calendula carotenoids have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help reduce gum swelling and promote wound healing in the oral cavity.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers are the most commonly used part due to their high content of anti-inflammatory and antiseptic compounds that help reduce gum swelling and promote healing.
Leaves: The leaves contain flavonoids and terpenoids that contribute to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are beneficial for treating gum inflammation and infections.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30-40 calendula officinalis flowers with bright orange petals on a sunny day with low humidity.
Dry the flowers in a single layer on paper towels for 2-3 days or use a food dehydrator at 95°f for 6 hours.
Infuse 2 tablespoons of dried flowers in 2 cups of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil at 150°f for 2 hours.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a glass container and discard the solids.
Mix 1/4 cup of the infused oil with 1/2 cup of beeswax and 1/4 cup of coconut oil in a double boiler at 180°f for 10-15 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis creams helps with gum swelling because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The gel extracted from the Aloe vera plant contains compounds like aloin and aloe-emodin, which reduce swelling and ease pain. The cream's cooling sensation helps to calm irritated tissues, promoting healthy blood flow and reducing inflammation around the gums. Additionally, Aloe vera's natural antibacterial properties prevent infections, allowing the gum to heal faster and reducing the risk of further swelling. This makes Aloe barbadensis creams a popular natural remedy for gum swelling.
remedy_constituents: Glucomannans: These soluble fiber constituents help reduce inflammation and swelling in the gums by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines.

Phenolic acids: These compounds, including caffeic acid and ferulic acid, exhibit anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate gum swelling by reducing oxidative stress and inhibiting the activity of inflammatory enzymes.

Anthraquinones: Specifically, aloin and aloe-emodin, these anthraquinones have anti-inflammatory and astringent properties that help reduce gum swelling by contracting blood vessels and reducing edema.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the most commonly used part of Aloe barbadensis due to their high concentration of soothing and anti-inflammatory compounds like aloe vera gel.

Gel (from Leaves): The clear gel extracted from the leaves is used to create creams for gum swelling because it has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that help reduce swelling and prevent infection.

Exudate (from Leaves): The exudate, a sticky substance secreted by the leaves, is also used in creams for gum swelling because it contains compounds that help reduce inflammation and promote healing in the affected area.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh aloe barbadensis leaves and cut them into small pieces for later use.
Weigh and combine 20g of distilled water with 20g of glycerin in a heat-proof bowl.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler at 70°c to 80°c for 15 minutes to dissolve ingredients.
Strain the mixture and add 50g of cold-pressed aloe barbadensis oil and 20g of beeswax to it.
Whip the mixture until it thickens and cools to room temperature for about 30 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea creams helps with gum swelling because of its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The cream contains compounds like alkylamides, caffeic acid, and rosmarinic acid that work together to reduce inflammation and combat bacteria that cause gum infections. By soothing and calming the gums, Echinacea purpurea creams can help alleviate swollen gums and promote a healthy oral environment. Regular use of the cream may also help prevent recurring gum infections and promote overall gum health.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid Glycosides: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling and inflammation in the gums.
Polysaccharides: They possess immunomodulatory effects, enhancing the body's immune response and potentially reducing the severity of gum swelling.

Flavonoids (specifically Cichoric Acid and Chlorogenic Acid): These polyphenolic compounds exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate gum swelling and protect against tissue damage.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Used for their anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory properties to reduce gum swelling.
Roots: Used for their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties to soothe and calm inflamed gums.
Leaves: Used for their antimicrobial properties to prevent infection and reduce gum inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of dried echinacea purpurea flowers at the peak of potency when flowers have faded.
Combine 1/2 cup of dried flowers with 2 cups of carrier oil in a clean glass jar.
Infuse the mixture in a warm place for 2-3 weeks shaking the jar daily to enhance potency.
Strain the oil through cheesecloth and discard the solids then transfer the oil to a clean container.
Mix 1/2 cup of the infused oil with 1/4 cup of beeswax and 1 tablespoon of vitamin e oil.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale creams helps with gum swelling because they contain the active compound gingerol, which has potent anti-inflammatory properties. This helps to reduce swelling and pain associated with gum inflammation. The analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties of Zingiber officinale also help to ease discomfort and promote healing. As a result, the cream can provide quick relief from gum swelling, making it a popular natural remedy for gum inflammation and other oral health issues. Its soothing effects also aid in gum regeneration.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: Gingerols have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and pain associated with gum inflammation.
Shogaols: Shogaols have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects that help alleviate gum swelling and prevent further irritation.
Terpenoids: Terpenoids in ginger have a cooling effect and help reduce inflammation, which in turn helps to reduce gum swelling and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are the most commonly used part of Zingiber officinale in creams for gum swelling due to their high concentration of gingerols and shogaols, which have anti-inflammatory properties.
Barks: Barks of the rhizome are also used to make creams for gum swelling due to their ability to provide a sustained release of active compounds, including gingerols and shogaols.

Stems: Stems of the rhizome are occasionally used, but less frequently than the rhyzomes and barks, as they contain lower concentrations of active compounds, but still provide some therapeutic benefits.
remedy_recipe: Extract 2-3 inches of fresh zingiber officinale roots and wash them under cold running water for 2 minutes.
Steam 1 cup of water in a saucepan for 10 minutes to create a base for the infusion.
Combine the steamed water with 1/2 cup of dried zingiber officinale roots in a heat-resistant container.
Let the mixture steep for 30-45 minutes in a warm place or until it reaches 80°f to 90°f.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean container to create the herbal infusion.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra creams helps with gum swelling because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce redness and discomfort. The saponins present in the cream, specifically glycyrrhizin, inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, thereby alleviating swelling. Additionally, glycyrrhizin's antioxidant properties help to protect the gums from oxidative stress, promoting overall oral health. The soothing and calming effects of Glycyrrhiza glabra creams also help to reduce pain and discomfort associated with gum swelling, providing relief to individuals suffering from gum inflammation.
remedy_constituents: Glycyrrhizin: Acts as an anti-inflammatory agent, reducing swelling and pain associated with gum inflammation.
Liquiritin: Exhibits antioxidant properties, which help prevent tissue damage and promote healing in gum inflammation.
Licoric acid: Demonstrates anti-inflammatory and anti-edematous properties, effectively reducing gum swelling and promoting healthy tissue regeneration.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are used due to their high content of glycyrrhizin, a compound known to reduce inflammation and swelling.
Leaves: They are used due to their anti-inflammatory properties, which help in soothing gum swelling.
Seeds: They are used due to their potential in reducing inflammation and promoting healing of the gum tissue.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30g of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots and clean them thoroughly in cold running water for 10 minutes.
Infuse 10g of the cleaned roots in 100ml of distilled water in a clean glass bowl for 4 hours.
Strain the infused mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container and discard the solids.
Mix the strained liquid with 10g of beeswax and 20g of sweet almond oil in a double boiler at 60 degrees celsius for 10 minutes.
Remove the mixture from the double boiler and whip it with a hand mixer until it thickens to a creamy consistency.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia creams helps with gum swelling because they contain tea tree oil, which has potent anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The oil's active compounds, such as cineole and terpinen-4-ol, work together to reduce swelling and combat bacterial infections that can cause gum inflammation. As a result, Melaleuca alternifolia creams can help alleviate symptoms of gum swelling, promoting a healthier and more comfortable oral environment. This natural remedy is particularly beneficial for individuals prone to gum inflammation or those experiencing gum recession.
remedy_constituents: Catechins: Catechins help with gum swelling by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress, thus alleviating swelling and discomfort.
Cineole (eucalyptol): Cineole helps with gum swelling by acting as an anti-inflammatory agent, reducing redness and swelling caused by irritation.
Linalool: Linalool helps with gum swelling by exhibiting anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling and alleviate pain associated with gum inflammation.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are used due to their high content of tea tree oil, which has antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce gum swelling.
Barks: The barks are used as they contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which help soothe and heal swollen gums.
Seeds: The seeds are used as they contain oil that has antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, which aid in reducing gum inflammation and swelling.
remedy_recipe: Distill 250ml of melaleuca alternifolia essential oil using a steam distiller for 4 hours.
Combine 500g of distilled water with 20g of beeswax in a double boiler.
Melt the beeswax mixture over low heat for 15 minutes while stirring constantly.
Add the distilled melaleuca alternifolia essential oil to the melted beeswax mixture in a ratio of 5%.
Pour the final cream mixture into sterile containers for storage and dispensing immediately.

remedies.remedy_desc: Arnica montana creams helps with gum swelling because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which effectively reduce pain and swelling in the affected area. The cream's active compounds, such as sesquiterpene lactones and flavonoids, have been shown to inhibit the production of inflammatory chemicals, thereby reducing the severity of gum inflammation. By reducing swelling and promoting healing, Arnica montana creams provide relief from discomfort and promote the recovery of healthy gums.
remedy_constituents: Sesquiterpene lactones: These compounds help reduce inflammation and swelling by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, thus alleviating gum swelling.
Flavonoids: Flavonoids in Arnica montana possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help minimize gum inflammation and promote healing.
Tremetol and inotropin: These terpenoid compounds exhibit potent anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects, which contribute to the reduction of gum swelling and pain.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers of Arnica montana are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of sesquiterpene lactones, which are responsible for the plant's anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties.
Leaves: The leaves of Arnica montana are also used to make creams for gum swelling due to their ability to reduce inflammation and promote healing.

Roots: The roots of Arnica montana are sometimes used to make creams for gum swelling as they contain a higher concentration of sesquiterpene lactones than the flowers, although the flowers are generally preferred.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-20 fresh arnica montana flowers and leaves on a sunny day in mid to late summer.
Dry the harvested material in a warm and well-ventilated area for 1-2 weeks.
Mix 10 grams of dried arnica montana with 20 grams of beeswax and 10 grams of coconut oil.
Melt the mixture in a double boiler over low heat for 10-15 minutes.
Stir the mixture and pour it into glass jars for storage and later use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Salvia officinalis creams helps with gum swelling because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce redness and pain associated with swollen gums. The cream's ability to soothe and calm irritated tissues promotes a healthy environment for gum healing. Salvia officinalis also contains antimicrobial compounds that combat bacterial infections contributing to gum swelling. By reducing inflammation and fighting infection, Salvia officinalis creams provide effective relief from gum swelling, promoting a speedy recovery and healthy gums. Its natural properties make it a popular choice for gum care.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic Acid: A polyphenolic compound that reduces inflammation by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, helping to alleviate gum swelling.
Ursolic Acid: A triterpenoid with potent anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce swelling and pain associated with gum inflammation.
Salvianolic Acid: A polyphenolic compound that exhibits strong anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities, contributing to the reduction of gum swelling and protection of gum tissue.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They contain bioactive compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce gum swelling.
Flowers: They contain essential oils with anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which contribute to soothing gum inflammation.
Stems: They contain salvinolic acids, which may help reduce inflammation and pain associated with gum swelling.
remedy_recipe: Harvest salvia officinalis leaves and flowers when in full bloom for optimal potency and flavor.
Steam distill the harvested salvia officinalis to extract the essential oil from leaves and flowers.
Combine distilled salvia officinalis essential oil with a carrier oil such as coconut oil.
Mix the salvia officinalis oil blend with a thickening agent like beeswax or candelilla wax.
Pour the salvia officinalis cream mixture into small jars and let it cool before storage.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe vera creams helps with gum swelling because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The gel extracted from Aloe vera contains compounds like aloe-emodin, aloin, and vitamins A, C, and E, which work together to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain. These properties help to calm swollen gums, promoting a faster recovery from gum inflammation and gum diseases. The natural antibacterial and antiseptic properties of Aloe vera creams also help to prevent infections and promote a healthy oral environment.
remedy_constituents: Apolypodol: It has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling in the gums by suppressing the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.
Aloin: This anthraquinone compound has analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate pain and swelling in the gums.
Furosesitol: It has been found to possess anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and inflammation in the gums by inhibiting the activity of various pro-inflammatory mediators.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They contain a high concentration of aloe vera gel, which is rich in anti-inflammatory and soothing properties.
Leaves: The gel from the leaves is also rich in vitamins, minerals, and amino acids that help to reduce swelling and promote healing.
Leaves: The aloe vera gel extracted from the leaves has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that help to combat infection and reduce gum inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 50g of aloe vera gel and 10g of coconut oil to mix together in a bowl.
Add 10g of beeswax and 5g of vitamin e oil to the aloe vera mixture and melt.
Stir in 20g of shea butter and 10g of jojoba oil to blend the mixture thoroughly.
Pour the mixture into a double boiler and heat until the beeswax melts completely.
Remove from heat and let the mixture cool and thicken before transferring it to a jar.

remedies.remedy_desc: Symphytum officinale creams helps with gum swelling because they contain the active compound allantoin, which has anti-inflammatory properties that reduce swelling and pain. The cream also promotes tissue repair and regeneration, helping to heal gum injuries and inflammation. Its soothing and calming effects ease discomfort, making it easier to manage gum swelling and promote a healthy gum environment. This natural remedy provides relief from gum pain and inflammation, allowing for a more comfortable and faster recovery.
remedy_constituents: Allantoin: It helps reduce inflammation and swelling in the gums by promoting healing and tissue repair, making it effective in soothing swollen gums.
Flavonoids: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which help to alleviate pain and swelling in the gums by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.
Saponins: They possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which contribute to reducing gum swelling and promoting overall oral health by protecting the gums from oxidative stress.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are commonly used due to their high concentration of allantoin, which promotes healing and reduces inflammation.
Buds: Buds are used because they contain a higher concentration of mucilage, a soothing agent that helps to reduce gum swelling and protect the tissue.
Roots: Roots are used as they contain a higher concentration of allantoin and other active compounds that aid in tissue repair and inflammation reduction.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 1 cup of dried herb in 2 cups of carrier oil in a clean glass jar for 2 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth into a clean bowl discard the solids.
Mix 1 tablespoon of beeswax with 2 tablespoons of shea butter in a double boiler.
Combine the infused oil mixture with the beeswax-shea butter mixture in the double boiler.
Pour the final mixture into clean glass jars and allow to cool and harden completely.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for gum swelling are topical treatments that utilize plant extracts to reduce inflammation and soothe swollen gums. These creams help alleviate discomfort and promote healing by utilizing the anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties of various herbs. The best herbal creams for gum swelling include Calendula officinalis, Aloe barbadensis, Echinacea purpurea, Zingiber officinale, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Melaleuca alternifolia, Arnica montana, Salvia officinalis, Aloe vera, and Symphytum officinale. If left untreated, gum swelling can cause pain, difficulty eating, and social embarrassment, negatively impacting daily life and overall well-being.
